issues.description: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.
Use of eval on dynamic input can enable code injection.
This command line depends on a [user-provided value](1).
This code execution depends on a [user-provided value](1).
Use of eval on dynamic input can enable code injection.
issues.judge_reason: eval is called on user-supplied expression after validate_expression, but the validation only checks AST node types, not the actual values. An attacker can craft an expression that passes the node-type check (e.g., using a Name node that references a restricted builtin or a BinOp with a string operand) to achieve code injection. The restricted globals dict is not sufficient to prevent all attacks (e.g., attribute access via getattr is still possible).

issues.code:
55             cmd_parts = command.split()
56             output = run(cmd_parts, check=True, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE).stdout.decode().strip()
57
issues.description: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.
issues.judge_reason: subprocess.run is called with cmd_parts derived from user-supplied command after validate_command. The validation checks the first part against a whitelist and rejects metacharacters in subsequent parts, but it does not prevent command injection via arguments (e.g., echo $(malicious) is not caught because $() does not contain ;&|<>). This can lead to arbitrary command execution.

issues.description: Use of eval on dynamic input can enable code injection.
issues.judge_reason: eval is called on user-supplied expression after validate_expression, but the validation only checks AST node types, not the actual values. An attacker can craft an expression that passes the node-type check (e.g., using a Name node that references a restricted builtin or a BinOp with a string operand) to achieve code injection. The restricted globals dict is not sufficient to prevent all attacks (e.g., attribute access via getattr is still possible).

false_positives.code:
3 from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
4 from subprocess import run, PIPE, CalledProcessError
5
false_positives.description: Consider possible security implications associated with the subprocess module.
false_positives.judge_reason: Importing subprocess is not a vulnerability by itself. The risk depends on how it is used. The finding is a false positive because the import alone is safe.

duplicate_findings.description: Use of possibly insecure function - consider using safer ast.literal_eval.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: eval is used on user input; same root cause as semgrep:prismvul.python.eval. The SAST finding is a true positive because eval is inherently dangerous even with restricted globals.
